ogTitle: Suttor vs Svensson Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Suttor and Svensson Heights.
heading: Suttor vs Svensson Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Suttor, Queensland and Svensson Heights, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Suttor has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Svensson Heights (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Suttor or Svensson Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Suttor has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Svensson Heights scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Suttor scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Svensson Heights scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Suttor or Svensson Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Suttor has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
